__fixture__.name: provider_refresh_replaced
__fixture__.scenario: A new provider.overlay_refresh arrives while a stale refresh is running. The queue applies the provider_refresh collapse_policy (replace_superseded): the stale refresh cancels at its next checkpoint and the new refresh starts from scratch. The collapse event records the superseded job id, the cancellation lag to the checkpoint, and a null checkpoint handoff (replace, not restart). collapse_count and replace_count increment; restart_count does not. The provider overlay surface projects warming until the new refresh reaches its first ready checkpoint.

checkpoint_handoff.note: Replace policy does not hand off a checkpoint; the superseded refresh's partial overlay is discarded after its cancel point.

note: Superseded refresh acknowledged cancellation at its next checkpoint in 420 ms; new refresh admitted immediately with warming projection on the provider overlay.
